Motion To Compel Outstanding Discovery In This Matter And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Continued To Sep-19-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Weltin, Esq. of the L/O of Daniel Weltin, T:510.856.4421.392.8400, for plaintiff and moving party;
Mark Meyer, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, T:510.213.4231, for defendant and opposing party.

Plaintiff's counsel did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, PLAINTIFF JOSE ANGEL's Motion To Compel Outstanding Discovery In This Matter And Request For Monetary Sanctions is continued to Sep-19-2019 at 9:30am in Department 302. Judge Pro Tem Jason Yurasek to prepare a Report and Recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

***After hearing, the parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.****

Judge Pro Tem Jason Yurasek issued his ruling on PLAINTIFF JOSE ANGEL's Motion To Compel Outstanding Discovery In This Matter And Request For Monetary Sanctions as follows:

Plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part. The request for sanctions is denied.

Plaintiff seeks documents not produced at deposition of one of Defendant's witnesses and owners, documents not produced in response to Plaintiff's second set of document requests, and a person most knowledgeable witness to testify about several of PMK topics. At hearing, counsel for Plaintiff simplified his requests to all pay stubs, time card records, time clock discrepancy logs and waivers for all kitchen staff (collectively the "Requested Records") from June 29, 2017 to the present, along with any updated policies or manuals as counsel made an offer of proof at hearing that certain policies were updated recently in August 2019 and additional discovery material was provided in the last thirty (30) days. Counsel for Defendant admitted that certain of these requested discovery material were produced, but only for the one (1) year period from October 2017 to October 2018.

Defendant is ordered to produce by no later than 5 p.m. pst Thursday, September 19, 2019 all pay stubs, time card records, time clock discrepancy logs and waivers for all Defendant's kitchen staff from October 2017 to August 31, 2019 that have not already been produced by Defendant or a third-party in this action. In addition, Defendant will provide a person most qualified for deposition for no more than 2 hours on the record on or before Friday, September 20, 2019 to testify only about the records produced for the first time in this action in compliance with this order. No other topics or questions outside of the records produced in response to this order shall be permitted.

The parties may modify this order by stipulation but must do so prior to any responsive date and only with the Court's approval, to be submitted to Judge pro tem Yurasek.

All other requests in Plaintiff's motion are denied.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek, not reported.

Motion To Compel Discovery; Notice And Motion
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Continued To Sep-19-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Meyer, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, T:510.213.4231, for defendant and moving party;
Daniel Weltin, Esq. of the L/O of Daniel Weltin, T:510.856.4421.392.8400, for plaintiff and opposing party.

Plaintiff's counsel did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, DEFENDANT THE BUNDOX RESTAURANT CORP. Motion To Compel Discovery is continued to Sep-19-2019 at 9:30am in Department 302. Judge Pro Tem Jason Yurasek to prepare a Report and Recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

***After hearing, the parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.****

Judge Pro Tem Jason Yurasek issued his ruling on DEFENDANT THE BUNDOX RESTAURANT CORP. Motion To Compel Discovery as follows:

Defendant's motion to compel is granted in part and denied in part. The request for sanctions is denied.

Defendant seeks the production of Plaintiff's telephone records sufficient to show all communications during work hours, and that personally identifying information may be redacted so long as the information provided is sufficient to identify messages, calls, and call length for non-work purposes during his employment while he was working for Defendant. Defendant further seeks all documents reflecting communications from Plaintiff to any other person during his working hours (including during any breaks or rest periods), again with the same clarification that certain information may be redacted to protect the privacy of third parties "so long as the content is for . . . non-work related purposes.") See Def.'s RFPs 12-13. Plaintiff objects that this information is not discoverable and also invades the privacy of Plaintiff and possibly third-parties.

Plaintiff argues that he and his fellow employees were not provided breaks and rest periods in this PAGA action. Defendant presented an offer of proof that witnesses will testify that not only were breaks and rest periods provided, Defendant was routinely seen using his phone, including taking calls and sending texts, while he took his breaks and rest periods. This information, if it exists, is clearly discoverable. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive materials no later than 5 p.m. pst Friday, September 20, 2019. Defendant may redact private information of third-parties and himself so long as the materials produced and redacted show the nature of the communications and whether they are work or non-work related.

Further, during his deposition, Defendant, on instruction of counsel, refused to provide the name of his telephone carrier and his telephone number. Those instructions not to answer were improper. Defendant will, for the reasons stated above, provide that information no later than 5 p.m. pst Friday, September 20, 2019.

Finally, Defendant asserts that the parties stipulated whereby Plaintiff would provide additional discovery responses to certain of Defendant's discovery requests. No stipulation was submitted, and the partial email snapshots submitted by counsel by declaration were not properly authenticated. Nevertheless, counsel for Plaintiff submitted a Declaration committing to provide supplemental responses to discovery. Since the matter was not properly before the Court and counsel for Plaintiff has promised to provide the supplemental discovery, the request is denied without prejudice to Defendant seeking relief should Plaintiff fail to produce the supplemental discovery.

The parties may modify this order by stipulation but must do so prior to any responsive date and only with the Court's approval, to be submitted to Judge Pro Tem Yurasek for consideration.

All other requests in Defendant's motion are denied.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek; not reported.
